author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you for the awesome idea!! 
That's the end of my head blender, and the start of a project!! I'm going to be charging a battery with this!
* I was just wondering if it's Ethical or not, to attach it to the hamsters wheel, Lol!
Kindest Regards,
Andrea, Jasper and George the Pigeon. XxX
* I also would like to note that the hamsters have loads of room, and a tunnel system to run about in, but they are mad about the wheel!!,🤔😁

author: @ianbaker6840
text: Rob can I give you an idea I've trying to get part to make a v.p.g (vortex pelton generator)by using a vortex fountain in theory it's possible genera the power. Yes you have to use a pump to push the water up but say you did not have space for a battery bank or super cappaster  this would be on demand power depending on your use for it

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text:
"but say you did not have space for a battery bank or super capacitor" 
oh there's Always space for those, people have been conditioned to just retail consume them for profit 
you just need to actually do the diy work & paint them on your device/paper substrate using rob's conductive ink as the primary material. see the vid, give it a go ian.

so pumped/ evaporated, air lifted venturi hydro with a U magnet stirrer

author: @David_Mash
text: If you had an unlimited constant heat source, how would you use it to generate residential electricity? Water flow? Air flow? Steam power? Peltier? Stirling?

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text: @McRootbeer i have been trying to find 10kw- 20kw stirling engines but no luck

replies.author: @McRootbeer
replies.text: @David_Mash what are you trying to power? A neighborhood?  The closest I've ever seen is a small company making a combined hot water/power system with a stirling, but that's more of a supplemental system than for primary power generation.

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text: @McRootbeer one 10 person home. We have solar to heat the sand thermal battery, we want something to take the heat 24/7 and power the home
